global.title: Violent tornado rips through North Texas area, leveling homes - CBS News
global.summary: A violent tornado tore through North Texas, leveling homes and leaving communities facing sudden loss and recovery needs.
global.whyItMatters: A pastor may want to pray for affected families and watch for ways churches can respond with relief, generosity, and trauma care.

global.title: US inflation jumps as fuel prices surge; economy grows in Q1 - Reuters
global.summary: Reuters reports U.S. inflation rose as fuel prices surged, even as the economy still posted first-quarter growth.
global.whyItMatters: Economic pressure shapes family stress, giving, and pastoral care needs; it also offers preaching connections around anxiety, provision, and stewardship.

global.title: Israeli military intercepts Gaza-bound aid flotilla - NBC News
global.summary: Israel intercepted a flotilla attempting to bring aid toward Gaza, highlighting ongoing tension around humanitarian access in the conflict.
global.whyItMatters: This keeps the humanitarian dimension of the Middle East crisis in view and can inform prayer, lament, and careful pastoral conversation.

canadian.title: Know your audience - Politico
canadian.summary: Politico's Canada Playbook says the Bank of Canada is warning a housing slump could curb growth, while wildfire preparation and national policy pressures remain in focus.
canadian.whyItMatters: Housing strain and wildfire readiness both affect congregational stability, community stress, and practical ministry planning in Canada.

canadian.title: Major film services company scales back operations as demand drops - NBC News
canadian.summary: A major film services company is cutting jobs and scaling back operations as production demand falls, signaling continued weakness in the screen industry.
canadian.whyItMatters: This matters in Canada because film and production work touches many households, especially in major urban centres, and may increase financial strain for some families.

local.title: This meteor shower in Metro Vancouver has 'long-streaking' shooting stars - Vancouver Is Awesome
local.summary: Metro Vancouver residents may be able to catch a meteor shower in early May, with fast meteors expected to leave long bright trails if skies are clear.
local.whyItMatters: This is a simple local moment of wonder pastors can use to invite awe, gratitude, and creation-focused reflection in a busy season.

persecutedChurch.title: Nun assaulted in Jerusalem as concern grows over attacks on Christians - The Catholic Herald
persecutedChurch.summary: A nun was assaulted in Jerusalem in what church leaders described as sectarian violence, amid wider concern about attacks on visible Christian presence near holy sites.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: This is a direct call to pray for the safety, dignity, and freedom of Christians in the land central to the gospel story.

persecutedChurch.title: Jewish leaders in London reveal safety concern after stabbing attack - CNN
persecutedChurch.summary: Jewish leaders in London are voicing renewed fears for community safety after a stabbing attack, reflecting rising concern over religiously targeted violence.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: Pastors should pay attention to threats against any faith community, both to pray well and to strengthen teaching on neighbor-love and resisting hatred.

leadership.title: How leaders can avoid falling short of staff expectations during transformations - Consultancy.uk
leadership.summary: This piece argues that change efforts often fail not because the idea is wrong, but because leaders hesitate, communicate vaguely, or fail key follow-through tests.
leadership.whyItMatters: Helpful for church leadership: people watch whether leaders embody change personally, not just announce it publicly.

goodNews.title: Celebrating 3 years of Axios New Orleans - Axios
goodNews.summary: Axios New Orleans marked three years by thanking readers and celebrating the shared trust, tips, and local connection that sustain community journalism.
goodNews.whyItMatters: It is a small but encouraging reminder that faithful local presence still matters and that communities are built through steady, relational work.
